author: jtraglia
content: Hey <@402080271563751435> just want to quickly mention that optimistic _might_ be deprecated in Gloas. I'm so behind on this discussion though, so I could be wrong. But this is something to keep in mind. See: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1496606725422190652

author: jtraglia
content: Hmm maybe not deprecated. I don't really know the status. 😅

author: jtraglia
content:
Also, please update the PR title to be a sentence in the imperative mood. The things I look for in a title:

> Titles must be written in the imperative mood. Titles must not have component prefixes like the "conventional commit" style. Titles should use sentence case, not title case. Titles must be fewer than 72 characters long. Titles should not be too vague. Words that are clearly code (functions, classes, etc) must be wrapped in backticks. There must be no terminal punctuation and ideally no punctuation at all. Titles must not contain double spaces, leading whitespace, or trailing whitespace.

author: jtraglia
content: ~~And no need to mention EIP-8025 in the title 🙂~~ Actually nevermind. This doesn't touch eip8025 specs and therefore doesn't have the label.

author: taulepton_
content:
Thanks for flagging this. It is certainly of interest, not only for EIP8025 but also for EIP8142. 

However, I don't interpret this a deprecation of optimistic sync but more so a modification of the engineAPI's shape / execution request commitments to support decoupled EL/CL sync.

I'll be sure to enter the conversation on this front as we will need to ensure that all unfinalized blocks are sent to the proof engine (as the proof engine has no alternative network to sync from). I'll prepare a small contribution but I think it can broadly be summarised by "if the node client is operating a proof engine then range sync (bypassing the proof engine) should not be used to sync above the finalized head" however I need to investigate the implications further before I have strong conviction in this statement.
